verbGlossary.ISSUED FOR: {source} formally released to coincide with an approved {target}, establishing the design basis for scheduled work
verbGlossary.GOVERNS: {source} defines the scope and technical requirements that {target} must execute
verbGlossary.DEPICTS SCOPE OF: {source} visually represents the physical work scope contained in a specific {target}
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} or document change initiates a tracked {target} change requiring re-sequencing or duration adjustment
verbGlossary.ASSESSED THROUGH: {source} {target} impact evaluated through a formal time impact analysis to quantify delay or acceleration
verbGlossary.SPECIFIES WORK IN: {source} defines the materials, methods, and quality standards for work performed in {target}
verbGlossary.PRESCRIBES: {source} dictates required lead times for procurement, curing, testing, or other sequenced work
verbGlossary.RESETS: Addendum changes shift a contractual milestone date established in the original {target}
verbGlossary.INCORPORATED INTO: Architect's bulletin or supplemental instruction folded into the next {target} with revised scope or sequencing
verbGlossary.CONSUMES: Outstanding RFI uses up available {target} while the team awaits a design clarification needed to proceed
verbGlossary.STALLS: Unresolved RFI halts progress on a critical-path {target}, threatening the project completion date
verbGlossary.BLOCKS: Open RFI prevents {target} from starting or continuing until the design question is resolved
verbGlossary.RELEASES: {source} removes the design hold, allowing the blocked {target} to proceed
verbGlossary.SHORTENS: Timely RFI response reduces the lag originally assumed in {target} logic for design clarification
verbGlossary.SCOPED WITHIN: {source}issued for work packages that fall within the look-ahead {target} planning window
verbGlossary.GATES: {source} approval required before {target} can begin, serving as a mandatory predecessor
verbGlossary.REQUIRES LEAD TIME IN: {source} needs to be submitted far enough in advance to respect the review and fabrication lag in {target}
verbGlossary.CLEARS: {source} response satisfies the gating requirement for {target}d milestone to proceed
verbGlossary.ADVANCES: {source} approval earlier than planned allows the next {target} to pull activities forward
verbGlossary.PRECEDES FABRICATION OF: {source} must be in hand before the fabrication activity on {target} can begin
verbGlossary.VALIDATES PROCUREMENT IN: {source} submittal confirms material specifications, allowing the procurement lead time in {target} to finalize
verbGlossary.APPROVED BEFORE: {source} submittal must receive approval prior to {target} that gates the related installation work
verbGlossary.IMPOSES: {source} establishes a contractual constraint that {target} must respect
verbGlossary.LOGGED AT: {source} receipt recorded at the schedule data date to establish {target} delivery timeline
verbGlossary.CATEGORIZES: {source} of delay formally classifies the delay event (excusable, compensable, concurrent) per {target} delay taxonomy
verbGlossary.SUBSTANTIATES: {source} of delay provides the contractual documentation required to support a time extension request
verbGlossary.DIRECTS: Change directive orders accelerated work or re-sequencing, compelling the contractor to compress {target}
verbGlossary.INITIATES: {source}triggers preparation of a recovery {target} to regain lost time or accommodate new scope
verbGlossary.REVIEWS: {source} convened specifically to review the look-ahead {target} and confirm near-term activity sequencing
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTS DECISIONS IN: {source} record {target}-related decisions, commitments, and action items from a schedule update discussion
verbGlossary.REPORTS PROGRESS ON: {source} captures actual work completed, crew counts, and weather conditions that affect {target} duration and progress
verbGlossary.RECORDED AS OF: {source} time-stamped to {target} data date for accurate period-by-period progress tracking
verbGlossary.NARRATES: {source} note provides a written account of delay circumstances that supports formal delay classification
verbGlossary.ANNOTATES: {source} note adds field-level context or commentary to a specific {target} progress record
verbGlossary.REDIRECTS: {source} changes the sequence or method of work for {target} based on field conditions
verbGlossary.NECESSITATES: {source} introduces a field change that requires a tracked {target} modification
verbGlossary.CONFIRMS: {source}log documents a verbal agreement about {target}, creating an auditable record
verbGlossary.PROMPTS: Plan revision notice triggers {target} cycle to incorporate the revised design information
verbGlossary.STRUCTURES: {source} organizes the recovery {target}s into a sequenced, accountable corrective-action framework
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO: {source} line item linked to a specific {target}, creating an accountable task with a due date
verbGlossary.TRACKED AGAINST: Project {target} progress measured against the scheduled duration and percent complete for the related activity
verbGlossary.VERIFIES PROGRESS IN: {source}provides visual evidence of work-in-place to corroborate reported {target} progress
verbGlossary.EVIDENCES COMPLETION OF: {source}documentation proves a milestone deliverable is substantially complete for {target} credit
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS START OF: {source}required before a milestone-gated {target} (excavation, occupancy) can begin
verbGlossary.IMPOSES HOLD ON: {source}creates a hard {target} that prevents downstream work from proceeding
verbGlossary.REGULATES: {source}plan dictates work procedures and sequencing requirements that constrain how {target} activities are performed
verbGlossary.RESTRICTS WORK DAYS IN: {source}document (SWPPP) limits available work days on the calendar due to seasonal or weather restrictions
verbGlossary.CLOSES OUT: {source}document must be completed and signed off before the substantial completion {target} is achieved
verbGlossary.RECONCILED WITH: {source}documentation compared against the as-built {target} to confirm that the record accurately reflects what was constructed and when
verbGlossary.DELIVERED AT: O&M manual handed over at the contractual milestone for owner occupancy or final completion
verbGlossary.ARCHIVED ALONGSIDE: {source}documentation filed together with the as-built {target} to create a complete project closeout record
verbGlossary.ERODES: Slow RFI response times progressively eat into available {target}, increasing schedule risk over time
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH RISK ON: High RFI volume per {target} indicates design uncertainty that elevates risk on the critical path
verbGlossary.CALIBRATES: {source} cycle time data used to set realistic lead-time assumptions in {target} logic
verbGlossary.FORCES REVISION IN: {source}submittal rejection rate compels {target} changes as resubmittal cycles extend procurement timelines
verbGlossary.DEGRADES: Frequent drawing revisions reduce {target} performance by introducing rework and re-sequencing
verbGlossary.INDICATES: {source} completion rate serves as a leading indicator of {target} health and field discipline
verbGlossary.SIGNALS: {source}are an early warning signal of {target} erosion and impending schedule risk
